verdict_reasoning: TMED10 is a type I single-pass TM p24-family cargo receptor predominantly resident in ER-Golgi cisternae, cycling between ER, Golgi, and PM as part of COPI/COPII vesicular trafficking. NCBI explicitly notes plasma membrane localization in addition to Golgi. The PM pool is a minority compartment arising from secretory cycling rather than stable surface residency, fitting dual_localization. Not a canonical surface receptor; no known cell-surface-directed therapeutic program. The ectodomain (GOLD domain) faces the lumen/extracellular face when at PM, so it is in principle accessible during the surface phase.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Magnitude and duration of PM surface pool is unclear; surface accessibility may be too transient for practical binder engagement.
